1.solution:
Effective rate: man=9/day, woman=7.5×2/3=5/day. Total work=(10×9+6×5)×18=120×18=2160.
→ With 10 men+9 women: 90+45=135/day. Days=2160/135=16.
Answer: 16.

4.solution:
When full, all open, net rate = −1/120 (cistern empties in 120 min).
→ 1/15+1/10−7/V=−1/120 → 1/6−7/V=−1/120 → 7/V=7/40 → V=40 L.
Answer: A (40 L).

5.solution:
168 taps in 4×3×7=84 plumber-hours → rate=2 taps/plumber-hour. Remaining=364-168=196.
→ 7 plumbers×7h/day×2=98 taps/day. Days=196/98=2.
Answer: 2 days.

7.solution:
In 3-day cycle: X+X+(X+Y+Z)=1/20+1/20+(1/20+1/30+1/60)=1/20+1/20+1/10=1/5.
→ 5 cycles=15 days completes the work.
Answer: C (15).

9.solution:
Total machine-hours = 14×12=168. After 4h: used 56. Remaining=112.
→ With 16 machines: 112/16=7 more hours. Total=4+7=11 hours.
Answer: B (11).

10.solution:
With leak, fills in 9h. Leak rate L=1/7−1/9=2/63 cisterns/hour.
→ Time to drain = 63/2 = 31.5 hours.
Answer: C (31.5 hrs).
